evidence.scientific_clarity: Dual-axis layout clearly shows loss decay (log) vs cosine LR schedule; color-coupled axis ticks/labels eliminate ambiguity as required by brief.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 is set and font is embedded (BMQQDV+DejaVuSans, CID TrueType); however, DejaVu Sans is the rcParams first choice and is actually used — brief discourages DejaVu Sans defaults, so -1.
evidence.palette: Blue (#0F4D92) for loss, red (#B64342) for LR — no green/red directional confusion; both are colorblind-distinguishable and semantically neutral for these series.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(7,4) with tight_layout and pad=0.8; image shows no excessive whitespace, no overlapping labels, y-ranges are data-driven (log loss, linear LR from 0).
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Legend inside upper-right, frameon=False; caption matches figure exactly (blue/red series, log left axis, linear right axis); no stray elements a reviewer would flag.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is genuine vector (Matplotlib PDF backend, 13 kB, 1 page); pdftotext extracts all text including axis labels and legend; font is fully embedded and subset.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (13 kB), figure.png (173 kB), figure.svg (39 kB), caption.tex (344 B), gen_figure.py (3.5 kB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption gives specific numerical values (loss ~2.19→~0.21, LR 1e-3→0 over 200 steps), identifies series by color and axis, and notes color-coupling design choice.

overall_strengths: Color-coupled axis ticks, labels, and spines to their respective series — directly fulfills the brief's hint and eliminates dual-axis ambiguity without a redundant legend.
All output formats produced with genuine vector PDF (fonttype=42, embedded fonts), selectable text confirmed, and a quantitatively informative caption.
overall_weaknesses: DejaVu Sans is the active font despite the rcParams listing Helvetica/Arial first — the system fell back to DejaVu Sans, which the brief flags as a default to avoid; specifying a font that is actually installed would fix this.
No minor x-ticks or grid lines to help readers read off step values precisely; a subtle grid or minor ticks on the x-axis would improve readability at no cost to ink density.
